flags.concern: Track reordering: Replacing the arrow buttons with a drag-only handle removes the current keyboard-accessible reorder path, and the plan's drag setup only mentions `PointerSensor`.
flags.evidence: Current reorder controls are keyboard-focusable buttons in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TrackLabel.tsx:196-220`. The plan removes them in Step 5, while Step 7 proposes a nested `DndContext` with `PointerSensor` only, even though the app-level context already wires a `KeyboardSensor` in `src/app/App.tsx:47-53`.

flags.concern: Track DnD: Over-engineering: the nested `DndContext` is justified as protection from clip-drag conflicts, but the repository's timeline clip dragging is not using dnd-kit, and the app-level dnd handlers already no-op for unrelated drags.
flags.evidence: Timeline clip dragging is implemented with raw pointer listeners in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts:83-120`, not via dnd-kit. The app overlay only renders when drag data contains `imageUrl` in `src/app/hooks/useAppDndOverlay.tsx:53-71`, and `useAppExternalDrop` exits early when drag data is missing or unrelated in `src/app/hooks/useAppExternalDrop.ts:74-85`.

flags.concern: Track reordering: The plan relies on manual validation only and does not add automated tests for the same-kind boundary rules or save/load persistence.
flags.evidence: The current defect is in pure reorder logic at `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:286-308`, and persistence also depends on load-time regrouping in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:320-333`. This repo already has vitest coverage for adjacent video-editor serialization logic in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/serialize.test.ts:1-58`, so omitting tests leaves the subtle boundary behavior unguarded.
